title: Rivian 2025 Gross Margin Expectations and Operating Leverage
text: Looking ahead, Rivian said it plans to achieve another modest gross profit in 2025. There are some offsets to cost reductions as management considers potential policy related impacts or demand impacts embedded within guidance. Additionally, the company will no longer have meaningful lower of cost or net realizable value benefits in 2025 given the improvements that have been made in underlying cost structure. The company has made significant progress in reducing R1's cost of goods sold by resourcing over half of the bill of materials. For R2, the company projects the bill of materials to be about half of R1's, with more than a 50% reduction in non-bill material COGS. This substantial cost reduction roadmap positions Rivian for improved profitability as volumes scale. The R2 platform represents a major step function improvement in cost efficiency. Production efficiency gains from manufacturing upgrades continue to contribute. Volkswagen partnership benefits are beginning to flow through supply chain efficiencies. Full year 2025 guidance incorporates these improvements while acknowledging macroeconomic uncertainties.
